title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Wellesley senior Emmie Laudano, 18, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Tierney Learning Center.
Laudano has trained for the marathon through the Dreamfar program and practiced on Heartbreak Hill each Saturday.
Funds raised by Laudano will support educational tools and services for children at the Boston-based center.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com launches a Greater Boston news quiz to test local news knowledge.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, has been with the site since 2019.
The quiz covers the latest trending stories in Greater Boston.

title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins lost 2-1 to a severely shorthanded Florida Panthers at Amerant Bank Arena.
Bruins head coach Marco Sturm criticized the team's lack of respect towards the Panthers, who are Stanley Cup champions.
Despite Bruins' recent success against top NHL teams, their loss to Florida impacts momentum towards the playoffs.

title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied, 40, was sentenced to eight months in prison for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts.
Bied pleaded guilty to smuggling wildlife parts from dealers in Cameroon and Indonesia into the United States.
He was ordered to pay a $75,000 fine and serve two years of supervised release post-prison.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2023 stabbing of Milteer Hendricks in Falmouth.
Black, then 22, attacked Hendricks with a group, stabbing him before fleeing in a black BMW, officials said.
Hendricks died after being med-flighted to Brigham Women's Health; Black faces a life sentence without parole.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia was suspended with pay after being named in a report on Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged drunk driving.
Sgt. Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide while under the influence, causing Angelo Schettino's death.
DeLucia allegedly urged a DA's security director to withhold information about Quigley's blood alcohol level from the crash.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adams couple sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter after foster child's 2020 death from untreated medical conditions.
Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker convicted of involuntary manslaughter and reckless child endangerment.
Sentences staggered due to couple's four other children; barred from future foster caregiving roles.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, sentenced to life without parole for poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in 2022.
Fowler died of ethylene glycol poisoning after drinking tainted Powerade; Church plans to appeal her conviction.
Prosecutors described Church and Fowler's relationship as 'toxic'; Fowler was involved with another woman.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox suffer worst season start since 2019, losing five of first six games, including a sweep by the Astros.
Boston's starting rotation struggles, posting a 5.22 ERA, while the team ranks last in home runs allowed per nine innings.
Key players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story have underperformed, contributing to the team's poor offensive showing.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, announced his NFL retirement after a 13-season career, including four years with the New England Patriots.
Gilmore played for six franchises and won a Super Bowl with the Patriots in 2018, earning Defensive Player of the Year in 2019.
He concluded his career with 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions, with two postseason interceptions in 2018.
